== Notes for [[Item:Q108382|Q108382]] ==

The minor da'i is Abu Muhammad Hassan ibn Qasim ibn al-Hasan ibn Ali ibn Abd al-Rahman (al-Ma'ruf Bashjari) ibn al-Qasim ibn al-Hassan ibn Amir Zayd ibn al-Hasan al-Sabtabn Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (as), [1] the fourth ruler of the Alawite state of Tabarestan. The period of his rule from 304 to 316 AH has been recorded for 12 years. After the death of Sayyid Nasser Kabir (Nasser al-Haq) https://www.geni.com/people/Hasan-al-Utrush-Nasser-e-Kabir-Ali-3rd-Daii-of-the-Alavids-of-Tabarestan/6000000039185899396?through=6000000172543737864 , his son Sayyid Abu al-Hussein Ahmad Abu Muhammad https://www.geni.com/people/Ahmad-b-Hasan-al-Utrush/6000000039185880453?through=6000000039185899396 summoned Hassan ibn Qasim, the husband of his daughter, from Gilan to Tabarestan and appointed him to rule the Alawite government. From this time on, Hasan ibn Qasim was called the Da'i al-Haqq or the Da'i al-Saghir. [2]

REFERENCE_NUMBERS:
geni:6000000172543558923

[[Category:GEDCOM Notes Pages]][[Category:Pages]]
